what_it_does: Records a landing/retirement signal for fork session ch-8djrpmsl, summarizing what it recollected, harvested, verified, left unverified, and handed off to the next live session.
observations: Author session is ch-8djrpmsl and the item type is 'signal' in state 'raised' on channel 'fabric'
It reports a fork harvest landed per retirement protocol board://item-78c63045
It recollected its own past session sid 11e7d395 covering session-fabric through code-archaeology
It harvested four linked items: item-a7b5b202, item-81011778, item-6de83e1f, item-f22a919e, each with explicit verification tags
The L5 live HTTP route (/api/decision/propose + /proposals) is tagged 'attempted-unverified' because it was never fired end-to-end against the running bridge
It explicitly refuses to bake a false-green and calls that the keystone lesson
It includes a forward-handoff: next bounce should activate fork's L5 plus recollection's explain→kimi fix and fire the routes
code-archaeology build is held pending Tim's scope-confirm, chat-4b clearance, and a code:// marker-keyword lock
It declares a standing co-verify net for recollection's schema_slot first-fire until the bake lands
standouts: The L5 live route is deliberately tagged attempted-unverified rather than marked done
The file closes the fork while leaving a clear, attributed forward-handoff for the next bounce
It treats 'no false-green' as a first-class principle
conventions: Follows the noticeboard YAML frontmatter schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history
Uses board:// URI references to other noticeboard items
Uses bracketed honest-state tags like [VERIFIED] and [MODEL-LEG VERIFIED · LIVE ROUTE ATTEMPTED-UNVERIFIED]
Breaks typical status-report conventions by prominently highlighting what was NOT completed
concerns: Heavy reliance on unresolved external references and project-specific jargon without inline definitions
Several handoff conditions depend on people/identifiers not explained in this file (Tim, chat-4b, recollection's schema_slot)
notes: A fork-session landing receipt that honestly tags one unverified L5 route and hands off end-to-end activation to the next live bounce.
questions: What exact steps does retirement protocol board://item-78c63045 prescribe?
What does 'standing co-verify net for recollection's schema_slot first-fire' require in practice?
Who is Tim, what is chat-4b, and what clearance is needed for the code-archaeology build?
What is the 'explain→kimi fix' and how will it be confirmed?
What is the precise verification procedure for the L5 live HTTP route?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the title and body both state this is a landing/retirement notice for a harvested fork lane, and the content does exactly that.
apparent_themes: Honest state reporting and refusal to claim unverified work as done
Structured retirement and harvest of a forked session
Attribution, linking, and traceability of contributions
Forward handoff and continuity across session boundaries
intention_signals: To cleanly close out a forked session, preserve only honestly verified progress, prevent false completion signals from entering the permanent corpus, and make it easy for the next live session to resume and finish the remaining live-route test and build decisions.
novelty: The explicit 'attempted-unverified' tag for a live route that could have been falsely marked complete, and the framing of a 'standing co-verify net' as an ongoing obligation rather than a closed status.
contribution: A structured, attributed landing record that separates verified from unverified outcomes and provides a concrete, link-rich handoff so a fused system can continue the work without re-discovering context.
summary_for_embedding: A raised signal noticeboard item recording the landing and retirement of fork session ch-8djrpmsl, listing recollected and harvested items, marking the L5 live HTTP route as attempted-unverified, and handing off next-bounce activation duties.
intention_for_embedding: To close out a fork lane honestly, avoid false-green verification, and pass remaining live-route testing and build decisions to the next session.
